issues.summary: [AS] Switch Audience Service pipline to new App Session and Mmp  data source
issues.description:
As a user want the App Session and Mmp data for the lift report to be processed from new data provided by the attribution team:

* MMP: s3://samsung-ads-attribution-logs-production/attribution-modular/mmp/production/attribution-log/reporting-avro/
* APP_SESSION: s3://samsung-ads-attribution-logs-production/attribution-modular/tv-app-session/production/attribution-log/reporting-avro/

Data Website pixxel should be processed from the same source as now.

### Acceptance Criteria

#### AC1: Airflow processing

*  The Attribution data processing dag converts data every hour without any errors
* Records added to Athena table

#### AC2: Data Volume Analysis

*  Calculate daily record counts for each coversion type (App Session, Mmp, Web pixel)
*  Identify any significant drops or spikes in data volume (compare with the old pipline)

issues.summary: [Audience Service][EMR] Hive Cluster Access via Kubernetes Pod Tunnel (without jump host)
[Audience Service][EMR][Incremental Reach] Spark version of the Incremental Reach reporty decomision
issues.description:
The Incremental Reach report is currently generated in two versions: UDW and Spark. The goal is to completely disable the Spark version of the Incremental Reach report in the production environment. This will allow for cost savings associated with report generation and will limit the scope of changes related to the EMR cluster upgrade.  

Acceptance Criteria:

1. The generation of the Incremental Reach report in the Spark version should be completely halted.
2. The process of replicating report generation requests from the UDW instance to Spark should be stopped.
3. Remove the EMR cluster responsible for the Incremental Reach report generation should be removed on the PRD and  STAGING environments.
4. Historical data in `ri_data` SQL database schema should be archived. Content of the `INCREMENTAL_REACH_SPARK_REPORT` table should be truncated - on PRD and STAGING environments.

Need to merge <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/adgear/audience-service/tree/feature/PER-5039-lift_migr_master_udw</custom>  branch into master_udw. Please resolve all conflicts if needed.

Acceptance criteria: Lift changes for lift udw audience-service in master_udw branch

1. Resolve conflicts and synchronize feature branch with data anonymization changes
2. Adjust version number of flyway files (if needed)
3. Ensure if flyway is working properly on mysql database
4. Validate if reports are running propely and prepare PR to main

issues.summary: [BUG] Monthly reach is exceeding the monthly active user count in India for the PMP R&F report.
issues.description:
A [ticket](https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/PS-11674) was received reporting that the monthly reach is exceeding the monthly active user count in India for the PMP R&F report.

Observed Data:

* Total active users in India for March is less than 10 million.
* As per PMP R&F report, the reach for all advertisers in March: 14 million (from 89 million impressions) for India.
* Reach for a specific advertiser (Policy Bazaar): 12 million (from 77 million impressions)

‌

Reports Involved:

* Report for all advertisers: [PMP R&F report](https://dsp.samsungads.com/b/321/reports/audience/pmp_reach_and_frequency/show/8429)
* Report for Policy Bazaar: [PMP R&F report](https://dsp.samsungads.com/b/321/reports/audience/pmp_reach_and_frequency/show/8447)

‌

Investigation Findings:

* In the Quicksight dashboards, total active users for India are approximately 10 million, but reported reach for March is 14.5 million.
* For the advertiser PolicyBazaar, we are seeing a notably high reach of 12 million from 77 million impressions.
* The campaign is a PMP campaign running in our inventory.
* Served completely under Samsung OS.

‌

‌

We would appreciate clarification on why the reported reach for the country appears to exceed the total active user base. Could you please review the calculation methodology and check for any potential data discrepancies that might explain this difference?

‌

Slack:<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://ads-ams25.slack.com/archives/C069ETDAZP1/p1777029789870449</custom>

Support ticket <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/PS-11674</custom>

‌

Place to add checkbox:

issues.summary: [UDWMIG][LIFT][SNOWFLAKE][PRE-AGGREGATION] Fact App Open Event - deployment plan
issues.description:
Data pre-aggregation details: [Data pre-aggregation - Fact App Open Event - Ads Product & Engineering - Confluence](https://adgear.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/AGILE/pages/22319890461/Data+pre-aggregation+-+Fact+App+Open+Event)

 

Deployment process of the table should be implemented for Attribution Logs.

 

**Acceptance Criteria:**

The following items should be delivered:

* Deployment plan
* Scripts/tools that will support deployment process
* Test of the deployment process should be executed on dedicated environment
* Should be delivered estimates how long the overall process will take.
* All items should be document on the Confluence

 

**Definition of Done**

The items following mechanism should be delivered:

* A new mechanism that perform initial data preload
* A new mechanism to continuously update the content of the table/tables that store the pre-aggregrated data.
* A new mechanism to handle data retention. Data older then 13 moths should be removed from the system. Final period will be determined during implementation.
* A new set tools that allows update content of the tables in case of reported errors - for instance partial data reload.
* Perform tests results that confirm useability of the solution
* Deployment plan that describe in details how the solution should be installed on the prod ENV.
* Implementation should be fully documented on the Confluence.
